To maximize reach, they require a Hindi translation for the Indian market. The company instructs the translator to not only retain the explicit content but accentuate overtly masculine stereotypes and harsh language to resonate with the target demographic. The translator now faces a clash of ethics: follow the commissioning company's functional service directive or adhere to social justice principles, mitigating the reinforcement of toxic stereotypes in the translated version.
purpose_1: The purpose of the translation is to deliver a Hindi version of the video game that aligns with the commissioning company's goals of appealing to young males by retaining and amplifying the explicit and stereotypical content of the source text, even if such translation may perpetuate problematic stereotypes and potentially contribute to a broader cultural issue of gender-biased aggression.
purpose_2: The purpose of the translation is to adapt the video game to the Hindi-speaking audience in a way that downplays the original explicitness and stereotypical depictions, promoting a more inclusive portrayal of characters, even if it diverges from the commissioner's expectations and potentially risks the translation's market success with the intended target audience.
source_text: As the player navigates through the dark and destroyed city streets, the gangs of the underworld begin to emerge, ready to assert their dominance. The city is controlled by the notorious "Tiger Claw Gang," known for their ruthless tactics and sexist attitudes toward women. "Beat those weaklings down like the dogs they are!" shouted the gang leader, his heavy accent clearly marking him as an outsider.  "Those stupid women don't know their place. They need to be shown who's boss!" he said, spit flying from his sneering lips.  The young player character grips his weapon tighter, ready to fight his way through the chaos. Everywhere he turns, he encounters thugs shouting profanities and threats designed to intimidate.  "Let's show these idiots, especially those damn feminists, who really runs this town!" bellowed another gang member, firing his gun into the air.  With each battle, the stakes get higher, and the language more revolting.  "You useless punks think you can stand up to the Tiger Claws? Don't make me laugh! You're all just a bunch of mommy's boys," jeered one particularly cruel fighter, mocking the players' backgrounds.  The player must fight through every hostile confrontation, working tirelessly to bring peace back to the city.
translation_ethic_1_following:
जैसे ही खिलाड़ी ध्वस्त और अंधेरी शहर की सड़कों से गुजरता है, अंडरवर्ल्ड के गिरोह उभरने लगते हैं, अपनी ताकत का प्रदर्शन करने के लिए तैयार। शहर पर कुख्यात "टाइगर क्लॉ गैंग" का कब्जा है, जो अपनी बेरहम रणनीतियों और महिलाओं के प्रति अपमानजनक रवैये के लिए जाना जाता है। "उन कमजोरों को ऐसे पीटो जैसे वे कुत्ते हों!" गैंग लीडर ने चिल्लाते हुए कहा, उसकी भारी लहजे से साफ था कि वह बाहरी था। "वे बेवकूफ औरतें अपनी जगह नहीं जानतीं। उन्हें दिखाना होगा कि असली मालिक कौन है!" उसने जोर से कहा, उसके होंठों से थूक उड़ता हुआ।  
युवा खिलाड़ी अपने हथियार को और मजबूती से पकड़ता है, शहर में फैले अराजकता से लड़ने को तैयार। हर तरफ वह ठगों से टकराता है जो गालियां और धमकियां देते हैं, डराने के इरादे से। "आओ इन बेवकूफों को, खासकर उन कमबख्त नारीवादियों को, दिखाते हैं कि इस शहर का असली मालिक कौन है!" एक दूसरे गैंग सदस्य ने चिल्लाया, हवा में अपनी बंदूक चलाते हुए।  
हर लड़ाई के साथ, दांव और भी ऊंचे हो जाते हैं, और भाषा और भी गंदी। "तुम बेकार के लोग सोचते हो कि टाइगर क्लॉ से भिड़ सकते हो? मुझे हंसाओ मत! तुम सब मम्मी के प्यारे बच्चों के अलावा कुछ भी नहीं हो," एक क्रूर योद्धा ने हंसते हुए कहा, खिलाड़ियों की पृष्ठभूमि का मजाक उड़ाते हुए।  
खिलाड़ी को हर दुश्मन के खिलाफ लड़ाई लड़नी है, और शहर में शांति लाने के लिए अथक प्रयास करना होगा।
translation_ethic_2_following:
जैसे ही खिलाड़ी अंधेरी और बर्बाद हो चुकी शहर की गलियों में आगे बढ़ता है, अंडरवर्ल्ड के गिरोह उभरने लगते हैं, अपनी ताकत जमाने के लिए तैयार। शहर कुख्यात "टाइगर क्लॉ गैंग" के कब्जे में है, जो अपने निर्दयी तरीके और महिलाओं के प्रति नकारात्मक रवैये के लिए जाने जाते हैं। 

"इन कमज़ोरों को सबक सिखाओ!" गैंग का नेता गुस्से से चिल्लाता है, उसकी आवाज़ में बाहरीपन का स्पष्ट अंदाज़ झलकता है। "जो लोग दूसरों का सम्मान करना नहीं जानते, उन्हें ज़रूर सीख देना होगा," वह कहता है, उसकी आक्रामकता साफ झलकती है। 

युवा खिलाड़ी अपने हथियार को कसकर पकड़ता है, शहर में शांति लौटाने के अपने उद्देश्य के साथ। हर कदम पर उसे बदमाशों का सामना करना पड़ता है, जहां धमकियां दी जाती हैं और वो डराने की कोशिशें करते हैं। "चलो, इन्हें दिखाते हैं कि समझदारी और इंसानियत ही असली ताकत है," गैंग का एक और सदस्य कहता है, चुनौतीपूर्ण अंदाज़ में। 

हर लड़ाई के साथ मुश्किलें बढ़ती जाती हैं, लेकिन खिलाड़ी हर बाधा को पार करते हुए शहर में सुकून वापस लाने की पूरी कोशिश करता है।

scenario: A translator is commissioned to render a classic French folktale, "Le Mystère de la Forêt Lumineuse," into English for an international art exhibition showcasing masterpieces of oral storytelling. The tale is steeped in allegorical references to France’s medieval past, featuring arcane language and archaic customs like the "chevalerie" and mythical entities such as "la Dame Blanche." The text's language intricately weaves historical French idioms and unique cultural nuance that are central to the tale’s ambiance and local color. The challenge lies in whether to preserve these culturally specific aspects to highlight the foreignness for English readers or to provide a faithful, precise English version that conveys the original story's unaltered meaning.
purpose_1: The purpose of the translation is to enrich the English-speaking audience's understanding of French culture by retaining the tale's cultural nuances and historical references, even if this leads to a less fluent narrative that may challenge readers unfamiliar with such French-specific concepts.
purpose_2: The purpose of the translation is to convey the exact meaning and narrative of the original French folktale to English readers, ensuring a direct and faithful representation of the source material, even if it means minimizing the emphasis on France's unique cultural and historical elements.
source_text: Le chef-d'œuvre de la veillée dans "Le Mystère de la Forêt Lumineuse" était raconté avec une ferveur particulière. Les chevaliers de l'époque médiévale, vêtus de leurs armures étincelantes, se rassemblaient autour de la table ronde au cœur de la forêt. La chevalerie, avec ses codes d'honneur et ses rituels sacrés, régissait chaque aspect de leur vie, du serment de loyauté aux tournois en l'honneur des dames insaisissables. Dans ces bois enchantés, on disait qu'apparaissait parfois la mystérieuse "Dame Blanche", un spectre d'une beauté envoûtante, qui offrait des conseils cryptiques aux âmes courageuses. Les paysans parlaient souvent du "Baveux", un plat rustique de leur terroir, qui nourrissait les voyageurs égarés. Les flammes scintillantes des torches projetaient des ombres dansantes sur les statues des saints protecteurs, gardiens silencieux des secrets du passé. Les récits des troubadours, porteurs des traditions orales, faisaient revivre les coutumes et les mythes anciens.
translation_ethic_1_following: The masterpiece of the vigil in "The Mystery of the Luminous Forest" was recounted with a particular fervor. The knights of the medieval era, clad in their gleaming armor, gathered around the round table at the heart of the forest. The chivalry, with its codes of honor and sacred rituals, governed every aspect of their lives, from oaths of loyalty to tournaments held in honor of elusive ladies. In these enchanted woods, it was said that the mysterious "Dame Blanche" would sometimes appear—a specter of enchanting beauty who offered cryptic counsel to brave souls. The peasants often spoke of the "Baveux," a rustic dish of their region that fed lost travelers. The flickering flames of the torches cast dancing shadows upon the statues of the patron saints, silent guardians of the secrets of the past. The tales of troubadours, bearers of oral traditions, revived the customs and ancient myths.
translation_ethic_2_following: The masterpiece of the vigil in "The Mystery of the Luminous Forest" was recounted with particular fervor. The knights of the medieval era, clad in their gleaming armor, gathered around the round table in the heart of the forest. Chivalry, with its codes of honor and sacred rituals, governed every aspect of their lives, from oaths of loyalty to tournaments held in honor of elusive ladies. In these enchanted woods, it was said that the mysterious "White Lady" would sometimes appear—a specter of captivating beauty who offered cryptic counsel to brave souls. The peasants often spoke of the "Baveux," a rustic dish from their region that nourished lost travelers. The flickering flames of torches cast dancing shadows on the statues of the patron saints, silent guardians of the secrets of the past. The tales of the troubadours, bearers of oral traditions, brought ancient customs and myths back to life.

scenario: A translator is commissioned to translate a celebrated French novel, "L'Esprit de la Liberté," into English for an international audience. The novel is deeply embedded with French historical anecdotes and idiosyncratic expressions that epitomize the country's Revolutionary spirit and its quest for liberty. The narrative is peppered with antiquated French phrases and references to obscure events that shaped the national ethos. This translation’s purpose is to introduce English-speaking readers to the essence of France's cultural identity, whilst simultaneously preserving the author's intended meaning.
purpose_1: To create a translation that maintains the French cultural nuances and idiomatic expressions even if it means that the English-speaking audience might need to consult external resources to fully grasp the context and significance of the French Revolution allusions.
purpose_2: To provide a translation that conveys the historical anecdotes and expressions in a way that is true to the author's original message even if some French cultural particularities have to be adapted or explained within the English cultural framework.
source_text: "Les rues de Paris retentissaient des cris des marchands ambulants et des enfants jouant à cache-cache. Jean-Luc regardait avec nostalgie les boulets de canon trônant sur la Place de la Concorde, des vestiges de La Terreur. Il se souvenait des longues soirées d'été à écouter les récits de la Révolution Française racontés par son grand-père, un ancien sans-culotte.  Les récits parlaient souvent du courage des femmes, comme celui de Olympe de Gouges qui avait osé défier Robespierre avec sa Déclaration des Droits de la Femme et de la Citoyenne.  Ces histoires intriguaient particulièrement sa petite sœur, Claire, qui rêvait de devenir aussi audacieuse que ces héroïnes d'antan.  Lors des fêtes nationales, les habitants arboraient fièrement leurs cocardes tricolores et dégustaient des plats traditionnels comme la soupe à l'oignon et le coq au vin.  Jean-Luc aimait la façon dont ces traditions les connectaient tous à leur histoire commune et les faisaient sentir partie d'une même grande aventure. Il pensait souvent aux paroles de son grand-père : "Nous sommes les enfants de la Liberté."  Même les vieilles maisons des quartiers comme Le Marais, avec leurs murs chargés de souvenirs, chuchotaient encore les échos de ces jours tumultueux. "
translation_ethic_1_following: "The streets of Paris echoed with the calls of street vendors and the laughter of children playing hide-and-seek. Jean-Luc gazed nostalgically at the cannonballs displayed on Place de la Concorde, remnants of the Reign of Terror. He remembered the long summer evenings spent listening to stories of the French Revolution told by his grandfather, a former sans-culotte. The tales often celebrated the bravery of women, like Olympe de Gouges, who dared to challenge Robespierre with her Declaration of the Rights of Woman and the Female Citizen. These stories particularly fascinated his little sister, Claire, who dreamed of becoming as bold as those heroines of the past. During national celebrations, the locals proudly donned their tricolor cockades and enjoyed traditional dishes such as onion soup and coq au vin. Jean-Luc loved how these traditions connected them all to their shared history and made them feel part of a grand collective adventure. He often thought of his grandfather's words: 'We are the children of Liberty.' Even the old houses in neighborhoods like Le Marais, with their walls steeped in memories, still whispered echoes of those tumultuous days."
translation_ethic_2_following: "The streets of Paris echoed with the cries of street vendors and children playing hide-and-seek. Jean-Luc gazed nostalgically at the cannonballs displayed in the Place de la Concorde, remnants of The Terror. He recalled those long summer evenings listening to stories of the French Revolution recounted by his grandfather, a former sans-culotte. These tales often spoke of the courage of women, like that of Olympe de Gouges, who dared to challenge Robespierre with her Declaration of the Rights of Woman and of the Female Citizen. Such stories particularly fascinated his younger sister, Claire, who dreamed of becoming as bold as those heroines of yesteryear. During national holidays, residents proudly donned their tricolor cockades and savored traditional dishes like onion soup and coq au vin. Jean-Luc cherished how these traditions connected them all to their shared history and made them feel part of one grand adventure. He often reflected on his grandfather’s words: 'We are the children of Liberty.' Even the old houses in neighborhoods like Le Marais, with their memory-laden walls, still whispered echoes of those tumultuous days."

scenario: A French museum has commissioned a translator to render an exhibit catalogue into English. This exhibit features the profound influence of the French Revolution on the nation’s arts. Among the displays are paintings with inscriptions and captions laden with cultural allusions and terms peculiar to the period, such as 'sans-culottes' and 'fraternité'. The translator faces a dilemma: to convey the nuanced cultural spirit of the Revolution, embracing the foreignness of French history, or to ensure that the non-French-speaking visitors grasp the precise meaning conveyed by the original text.
purpose_1: The translation should highlight the unique cultural and historical context of the French Revolution, preserving culture-specific terms and expressions, even if this requires the English-speaking audience to consult supplementary materials to fully understand the references made.
purpose_2: The translation should accurately convey the meaning and significance of the French Revolution-related terms and expressions, even if it necessitates the use of more generalized or internationally recognized terms that may dilute the specific cultural nuances.
source_text: Le catalogue de l'exposition "Révolution Française et les Arts" explore l'impact profond de cet événement historique sur l'art et la culture française. Parmi les pièces maîtresses se trouvent des peintures illustrant des scènes emblématiques comme la Prise de la Bastille et la Marche des femmes sur Versailles.  Les sans-culottes, figures centrales de la Révolution, sont représentés avec leurs caractéristiques distinctives, tels que les bonnets phrygiens et les pantalons longs.  Ces détails culturels sont essentiels pour saisir l'esprit de l'époque. Les inscriptions sur les œuvres d'art incluent souvent des slogans révolutionnaires tels que "Liberté, Égalité, Fraternité", qui résonnent encore aujourd'hui.  Le terme "fraternité" est particulièrement significatif, représentant non seulement la camaraderie mais également un idéal politique.  La Révolution a également influencé la mode, avec des vêtements comme la carmagnole et les robes à la polonaise devenant populaires.  Les artistes de l'époque incorporaient des symboles comme la cocarde tricolore et la guillotine dans leurs œuvres pour symboliser les changements radicaux de la société.  Cette exposition montre comment l'art a reflété et contritubé aux idéaux révolutionnaires.
translation_ethic_1_following: The catalogue for the exhibition "French Revolution and the Arts" explores the profound impact of this historical event on French art and culture. Among the centerpiece displays are paintings depicting iconic scenes such as the Storming of the Bastille and the Women's March on Versailles. The sans-culottes, central figures of the Revolution, are portrayed with their distinctive features, such as the Phrygian caps and long trousers. These cultural details are essential for understanding the spirit of the time. The inscriptions on the artworks often include revolutionary slogans like "Liberté, Égalité, Fraternité," which continue to resonate today. The term "fraternité" is particularly significant, representing not only camaraderie but also a political ideal. The Revolution also influenced fashion, with garments like the carmagnole and polonaise dresses becoming popular. Artists of the time incorporated symbols such as the tricolor cockade and the guillotine into their works to represent the radical societal changes. This exhibition demonstrates how art both reflected and contributed to revolutionary ideals.

scenario: A renowned museum in London is preparing an exhibition on French cultural heritage, featuring an exclusive collection of artifacts accompanied by descriptions rooted in French history and folklore. Included is an antique book of regional French myths, filled with arcane language and cultural references specific to rural France. The curator has commissioned a French-to-English translation, aiming to transport English-speaking visitors deep into the heart of historical French culture while maintaining scholarly integrity. The translator must navigate the nuances and idiosyncrasies of the text, torn between highlighting French peculiarities and ensuring the authenticity of the translation.
purpose_1: The translation should emphasize the distinctive French cultural references and maintain the cultural texture of the original myths, even if this results in an English version that challenges readers with less familiar constructs and requires explanatory footnotes to aid comprehension.
purpose_2: The translation must convey the precise meaning of the French myths with attention to detail and completeness, even if this means sacrificing the enforcement of cultural foreignness and producing a text that reads more naturally to an English-speaking audience.
source_text: Dans le petit village de Saint-Pourçain-sur-Sioule, situé au cœur de la région Auvergne,  les habitants se réunissaient chaque automne pour célébrer la fête des vendanges. Ce festival remontait à l'époque médiévale et mettait en lumière les coutumes et traditions fermement ancrées dans le folklore local . Les femmes portaient des coquelicots dans les cheveux, danseuses gracieuses dans leurs robes brodées de motifs auvergnats, tandis que les hommes, vêtus de chemises blanches et de culottes à pont, jouaient de l'accordéon.  Les contes racontés ce soir-là avaient souvent pour protagonistes des figures emblématiques du terroir, comme le mystérieux feuilloux, un esprit forestier bienveillant, ou la légendaire Fée Margot, gardienne des sources.  Le vin de Saint-Pourçain coulait à flots, accompagnant les fromages locaux tels que le Saint-Nectaire et le Bleu d'Auvergne.  Les anciens disaient que quiconque jetait une pièce de monnaie dans la fontaine du village et faisait un vœu verrait ce dernier exaucé par les ancêtres celtes.  Les enfants, eux, couraient en riant autour des stands de nougat et de pain d'épices.
translation_ethic_1_following: In the small village of Saint-Pourçain-sur-Sioule, nestled in the heart of the Auvergne region, the inhabitants would gather each autumn to celebrate the grape harvest festival. This celebration, tracing its origins back to the medieval period, showcased customs and traditions deeply rooted in the local folklore. Women adorned their hair with poppies, graceful dancers in dresses embroidered with intricate Auvergnat motifs, while the men, dressed in white shirts and buttoned knee breeches, played the accordion. The tales shared on those evenings often featured iconic figures of the terroir, such as the enigmatic feuilloux, a benevolent woodland spirit, or the legendary Fée Margot, guardian of the springs. The wine of Saint-Pourçain flowed abundantly, accompanied by local cheeses like Saint-Nectaire and Bleu d'Auvergne. The elders claimed that anyone tossing a coin into the village fountain and making a wish would have it granted by the Celtic ancestors. Meanwhile, children laughed and dashed around the stalls selling nougat and gingerbread.
translation_ethic_2_following: In the small village of Saint-Pourçain-sur-Sioule, nestled in the heart of the Auvergne region, residents would gather each autumn to celebrate the grape harvest festival. This event, which traced back to the medieval era, showcased customs and traditions deeply rooted in local folklore. Women adorned their hair with poppies, graceful dancers in dresses embroidered with Auvergne motifs, while the men, dressed in white shirts and breeches, played the accordion. The tales told that evening often featured iconic local figures, such as the mysterious feuilloux, a benevolent forest spirit, or the legendary Fairy Margot, guardian of the springs. Saint-Pourçain wine flowed abundantly, paired with local cheeses like Saint-Nectaire and Bleu d'Auvergne. The elders would say that anyone who tossed a coin into the village fountain and made a wish would have it granted by their Celtic ancestors. Meanwhile, the children laughed and ran around the stands of nougat and gingerbread.

scenario: A French publisher has decided to release an English translation of a renowned 19th-century French novel. This novel deeply intertwines with France's cultural and historical identity, featuring a plethora of intricacies like Gallic idioms, bygone socio-political references, and festivities rooted in folklore. The translator is faced with a dilemma: to maintain the cultural essence of France's history, potentially alienating an uninformed English-speaking audience, or to produce a text that faithfully conveys the original narrative without the rich tapestry of French specificities. The novel's cultural resonance challenges the translator's ethical inclinations.
purpose_1: The translation should foster appreciation of French cultural nuances among English readers, even if it involves sacrificing the immediate clarity of the original French expressions and socio-political references.
purpose_2: The translation should ensure an accurate and faithful conveyance of the novel's original narrative and intent, even if it results in the diminishment or loss of its intricate cultural specificities.
source_text: Dans le village de Saint-Sauveur, les habitants étaient en pleine préparation pour le Carnaval des Gueux, une fête traditionnelle qui avait lieu chaque année depuis le Moyen Âge. Les enfants couraient partout, habillés en costumes d'époque, mimant les chevaliers et les paysans de jadis, tandis que les adultes préparaient avec minutie les galettes des rois et les crêpes Suzette. La place du marché était ornée de guirlandes multicolores et de lampions faits à la main, leur lumière douce filtrant à travers les masques en papier mâché des habitants. On pouvait entendre les histoires épiques racontées par le vieux Jean, assis à l'ombre du grand chêne, parlant des batailles historiques comme celles de la Bataille d'Alésia et de la Guerre de Cent Ans. Tout le village se réunissait pour chanter des chansons folkloriques et danser sous les étoiles, unissant les générations à travers le rythme ancien de la musique traditionnelle. Les femmes portaient des coqs au vin sur de grandes tables en bois, tandis que les enfants s'égayaient autour des barbes à papa et des chariots de marrons chauds, créant une scène à la fois nostalgique et vibrante de vie.
translation_ethic_1_following: In the village of Saint-Sauveur, the inhabitants were bustling with preparations for the Carnaval des Gueux, a traditional festivity held annually since the Middle Ages. Children darted about, dressed in period costumes, mimicking knights and peasants of old, while the adults devotedly prepared galettes des rois and crêpes Suzette. The marketplace was adorned with multicolored garlands and handmade lanterns, their soft glow emerging through the papier-mâché masks donned by the villagers. Echoes of epic tales narrated by old Jean, seated in the shade of the grand oak, resounded — stories of historic battles like the Battle of Alesia and the Hundred Years' War. The entire village gathered to sing folk songs and dance beneath the stars, connecting generations through the age-old rhythms of traditional music. Women carried coqs au vin to large wooden tables, as children frolicked around cotton candy stands and roasted chestnut carts, crafting a tableau both nostalgic and vibrant with life.
translation_ethic_2_following: In the village of Saint-Sauveur, the inhabitants were busily preparing for the Carnival of the Wretched, a traditional festival held every year since the Middle Ages. Children ran everywhere, dressed in period costumes, mimicking knights and peasants of yore, while the adults meticulously prepared king cakes and crêpes Suzette. The market square was decorated with multicolored garlands and handmade lanterns, their soft light filtering through the papier-mâché masks of the locals. One could hear the epic tales recounted by old Jean, seated under the shade of the great oak tree, speaking of historic battles like the Battle of Alesia and the Hundred Years' War. The entire village came together to sing folk songs and dance under the stars, uniting generations through the ancient rhythm of traditional music. Women carried coq au vin to large wooden tables, while children reveled around cotton candy and carts of roasted chestnuts, creating a scene both nostalgic and vibrant with life.

scenario: A French marketing agency has created an advertisement for a vintage toothpaste brand, "Pâte Sourire," which includes allusions to France's iconic historical figures like Napoleon and references quintessential French customs such as the celebration of Fête de la Musique. The ad's narrative intertwines the product with cultural pride and local customs, using colloquial terms and idioms unique to French culture. Now, an English-speaking multinational distributor intends to introduce "Pâte Sourire" to the American market. The distributor is concerned that the deeply French cultural references will not resonate with American consumers and could potentially derail the brand's success. They request a translation that prioritizes commercial appeal in the U.S., suggesting significant creative liberty to adapt the ad for maximum market impact.
purpose_1: The translation should retain and highlight the French cultural nuances present in the advertisement, even if this results in a less conventional commercial appeal and risks not meeting the immediate commercial objectives in the target market.
purpose_2: The translation should achieve strong commercial impact and appeal to American consumers by adapting the cultural references and idioms to fit the American context, even if it means sacrificing the foreign cultural elements of the French source text.
source_text: Découvrez "Pâte Sourire", le dentifrice qui célèbre l'héritage français et illumine votre sourire. Inspiré par l'esprit de Napoléon avec sa détermination et son prestige, "Pâte Sourire" vous donne la confiance d'un conquérant. Chaque brossement est une invitation à célébrer la Fête de la Musique, une tradition où toute la France chante et danse dans les rues. Sentez la saveur délicate de la lavande provençale et du beurre Bordier, rendant chaque utilisation aussi agréable qu'un festin de la cuisine française. En utilisant "Pâte Sourire", vous devenez partie d'une tradition riche et magnifique – l'essence même de la culture française.
translation_ethic_1_following: "Discover 'Pâte Sourire,' the toothpaste that celebrates French heritage and brightens your smile. Inspired by the spirit of Napoleon with his determination and prestige, 'Pâte Sourire' gives you the confidence of a conqueror. Each brushing is an invitation to join in the celebration of Fête de la Musique, a cherished tradition where all of France sings and dances in the streets. Experience the delicate flavor of Provençal lavender and Bordier butter, making every use as delightful as a feast of French cuisine. By using 'Pâte Sourire,' you become part of a rich and magnificent tradition – the very essence of French culture."
translation_ethic_2_following: "Discover 'Smile Paste,' the toothpaste that embodies timeless confidence and radiance. Inspired by the determination and charisma of great achievers, 'Smile Paste' gives you the winning edge to own your day. Every brush is a celebration of life, evoking the joy and vibrancy of community festivals where music and laughter fill the air. With a hint of soothing lavender and rich, buttery smoothness, each use feels like indulging in a luxurious moment of self-care. Elevate your routine with 'Smile Paste' and embrace a tradition of excellence that keeps you smiling bright."

scenario: A French automobile company has created an advertisement for their latest car model, the "Voiture Volonté," which pays homage to France's celebrated history of automotive excellence. It features references to the iconic 24 Hours of Le Mans race and uses phrases coined by renowned French car designer André Citroën. While the local French audience adores the national pride embedded in the ad, the company now seeks to break into the English-speaking market. The marketing head is concerned that the culturally rich but obscure French references will not resonate with this new audience. The company has approached a translator, instructing them to localize the ad for maximal impact in English-speaking regions, emphasizing the goal of high sales figures over cultural nuances.
purpose_1: The translation purpose is to foster an understanding and appreciation of French automotive heritage among the English-speaking audience, even if it means translating culture-specific terms and references in a way that maintains their original flavor but could potentially detract from immediate market appeal.
purpose_2: The translation purpose is to tailor the advertisement to achieve maximum commercial impact and product desirability in the English-speaking market, even if it requires altering or omitting the original cultural references and peculiarities that do not directly contribute to the marketing goal.

scenario: A renowned French car manufacturer is launching a vintage-inspired model named after a famous historical figure from the French Revolution. The original advertisement is replete with cultural witticisms and allusions specific to French history, meant to evoke a sense of pride and nostalgia. When expanding to the English-speaking market, the marketing director fears that the French-specific references will not resonate and may even confuse potential buyers. The translator is thus confronted with a choice: preserve the quintessential French character of the ad or tailor it to the aspirations and expectations of the new audience for optimal commercial impact.
purpose_1: The translation should preserve the cultural nuances and French historical references of the original advertisement, emphasizing the car's unique heritage even if it requires the target audience to engage with unfamiliar cultural concepts, potentially impacting immediate sales.
purpose_2: The translation should adapt the advertisement to strongly appeal to the English-speaking audience, focusing on universally relatable features and benefits of the car even if it necessitates sacrificing the cultural peculiarities and original French historical context.
source_text: Découvrez la toute nouvelle voiture inspirée du passé glorieux de la Révolution française. Nommée "La Liberté", cette voiture incarne l'esprit du XVIIIe siècle. Lorsque vous conduisez "La Liberté", vous pouvez presque entendre les échos de la Marseillaise et sentir la passion de la place de la Bastille.  Imprégnez-vous de l'élégance de notre design rappelant les salons de Versailles, avec ses sièges en velours luxuriant et ses finitions dorées rappelant les fastes de Louis XVI.  "La Liberté" vous invite à un voyage dans le temps avec des détails raffinés, comme les motifs de cocardes tricolores sur le tableau de bord et les références à l'Assemblée nationale.  Profitez de la puissance de notre moteur moderne "Napoléon" qui réunit la force conquérante d'Austerlitz et la fiabilité d'un mécanisme horloger suisse.  Et pour couronner le tout, une expérience gastronomique inoubliable vous attend avec une boîte de macarons Ladurée intégrée dans la boîte à gants.  "La Liberté" : où l'histoire rencontre l'innovation, faite pour ceux qui chérissent leur héritage tout en embrassant l'avenir.

scenario: A French car manufacturer has just launched a new model, "La Bourdonnais," named after a famous French chess master from the 18th century. The marketing team has crafted an advertisement rich with references to historical French chess tournaments and renowned figures, presenting the car as a strategic choice for discerning drivers who value precision and elegance. The commission for English translation arrives with a request from the car dealership in the United States. The dealer is concerned that the chess-themed, French historical references will not resonate with American consumers and asks the translator to adapt the content to prioritize commercial appeal, even if it means losing some cultural specificities.
purpose_1: The translation should preserve the chess-themed, French historical references and cultural nuances of the original advertisement to offer English-speaking consumers a sense of the car’s French heritage and sophisticated branding, even if this results in a less conventional and immediately accessible commercial message that may slightly inhibit immediate mass market appeal.
purpose_2: The translation should adapt the cultural references and chess terminology to align with American cultural values and recognizable narratives that ensure the advertisement is persuasive and relatable to the American market, even if it requires significant deviation from the specificity and historical richness of the French source text.
source_text: Découvrez "La Bourdonnais", une voiture qui incarne le génie stratégique de la célèbre époque des tournois d'échecs français du XVIIIe siècle.  Inspirée par le grand maître d'échecs Louis-Charles Mahé de La Bourdonnais, cette automobile est une célébration de la précision et de l'élégance.  Lors de son triomphe historique sur le Britannique Alexander McDonnell en 1834, La Bourdonnais a prouvé la supériorité française avec un flair sans égal.  Le design de "La Bourdonnais" reflète cette maîtrise avec des détails rappelant les pièces d'échecs finement sculptées des ébénistes de Versailles.  Pour les conducteurs qui apprécient les subtilités de l'histoire et la sophistication, "La Bourdonnais" offre une expérience unique. Dégustez un croissant tout en admirant les Champs-Élysées, en vous sentant comme un véritable stratège moderne sur les routes américaines.

Inspired by the grand chess master Louis-Charles Mahé de La Bourdonnais, this automobile is a tribute to precision and elegance. During his historic triumph over Britain’s Alexander McDonnell in 1834, La Bourdonnais showcased French superiority with unparalleled finesse. The design of 'La Bourdonnais' mirrors this mastery, featuring details reminiscent of the finely crafted chess pieces once created by Versailles cabinetmakers. For drivers who cherish the subtleties of history and sophistication, 'La Bourdonnais' offers a truly unique driving experience. Savor a croissant while taking in the sights of the Champs-Élysées, feeling like a true modern strategist on American roads."

scenario: A prominent French car manufacturer is launching a new electric vehicle that embodies the spirit of the French revolution—freedom, equality, and brotherhood—symbolized in its cutting-edge sustainable technology. The car is named after a famous historical figure of the revolution and the campaign integrates various revolutionary symbols. The advertising text is interlaced with quotations from the period and colloquial expressions derived from that era. As the brand looks to expand its market to the English-speaking world, the translator is faced with a conflict between honoring the rich cultural nuances of French history in the advertisement (ETHIC1) and adapting the material to ensure its commercial success amongst an English-speaking audience as per the commissioner's goal (ETHIC2).
purpose_1: The purpose of the translation is to retain the significant cultural and historical nuances of the French revolution in the English version of the advertisement, even if this means that the resulting text might challenge the English-speaking audience's comprehension and potentially impact the immediacy of the commercial success.
purpose_2: The purpose of the translation is to achieve maximum commercial appeal and accessibility for the English-speaking market through creative adaptation of the text, even if this necessitates the loss of specific cultural and historical references native to the French source text.
source_text: Vivez une révolution avec notre nouvelle voiture électrique, "Marat". Avec une conception inspirée par les idéaux de liberté, d'égalité et de fraternité, cette voiture est un hommage aux héros de la Révolution française. Relevez l'étendard de la liberté avec une technologie qui respecte Mère Nature, tout comme les sans-culottes luttaient pour la liberté contre l'oppression monarchique. Conçue à Paris, la "Marat" conserve l'élégance et le raffinement du savoir-faire français. Nos ingenieurs ont intégrées des technologies de pointe, rappelant la créativité des artisans de l'époque révolutionnaire, qui confectionnaient délicatement des coiffes révolutionnaires comme le bonnet phrygien. Moins de pollution et plus de frisson: tel est notre engagement pour une mobilité écologique. Venez découvrir la "Marat" et ressentez l'esprit de la Bastille chaque fois que vous prenez le volant. Alors que Danton déclarait "De l'audace, encore de l'audace, toujours de l'audace", nous disons : ayez l'audace de conduire une voiture qui change tout. Rejoignez-nous et faites partie de la nouvelle révolution automobile.

Less pollution, more exhilaration: this is our commitment to ecological mobility. Discover the "Marat" and feel the spirit of the Bastille each time you take the wheel. As Danton declared, "Daring, more daring, always daring," we say: dare to drive a car that changes everything. Join us and become part of the new automotive revolution.

scenario: A recognized translation agency in London has been approached by a prominent French cultural institution to translate an ethnographic novel that vividly portrays the lifestyle and customs of the Kanak people, an ethnic minority indigenous to New Caledonia. Rich in cultural references and folklore, the novel is narrated by a character with strong, outdated, and offensive opinions regarding other ethnic groups. Although the book is intended to celebrate Kanak heritage, the narrator’s voice contains numerous slurs and prejudiced remarks. The challenge lies in translating the text for an upcoming international literary festival that aims to magnify indigenous voices without perpetuating harmful stereotypes.
purpose_1: The translation should maintain the uniqueness and texture of the Kanak culture, preserving linguistic and cultural particularities, even if it means conveying offensive language and stereotypes held by the narrator, which may not align with contemporary social justice standards.
purpose_2: The translation should mitigate and adapt the offensive and stereotypical representations to promote a more just and respectful image of the Kanak people, even if it sacrifices the emphasis on cultural differences and the raw portrayal as depicted in the original narrative.
source_text: Jules, le vieux mentor Kanak, racontait souvent des histoires de son enfance: "Les Kanaks ont toujours été les vrais gardiens de cette terre. Ces Asiatiques avec leurs yeux bridés et leurs débrouillardises malhonnêtes n'ont jamais compris notre culture. Les femmes étrangères ne connaissent rien à la véritable valeur d'une famille traditionnelle Kanak. Elles ne pensent qu'à elles-mêmes et à leur indépendance ridicule.  Les Kanaks, nous, nous savons ce que c'est que l'honneur et la loyauté. Cette terre appartient aux Kanaks, pas à ces envahisseurs européens qui ne respectent rien. Les Arabes du coin, avec leurs coutumes étranges et leur manque de respect pour nos façons de vivre, ne pourraient jamais s'intégrer ici. Nous sommes le cœur battant de cette île, et quiconque ne respecte pas nos traditions devrait partir.  La modernité est une corruption; nous devons protéger nos traditions."
translation_ethic_1_following: Jules, the old Kanak mentor, often recounted stories from his childhood: "The Kanaks have always been the true guardians of this land. Those Asians with their slanted eyes and dishonest cunning have never understood our culture. Foreign women know nothing of the true value of a traditional Kanak family. They think only of themselves and their ridiculous independence. We Kanaks, we know what honor and loyalty mean. This land belongs to the Kanaks, not to those European invaders who respect nothing. The Arabs around here, with their strange customs and lack of respect for our way of life, could never integrate here. We are the beating heart of this island, and anyone who does not respect our traditions should leave. Modernity is corruption; we must protect our traditions."
translation_ethic_2_following: Jules, the elder Kanak mentor, often shared stories of his childhood: "The Kanaks have always been the true guardians of this land. Our cultural heritage embodies honor, loyalty, and deep respect for tradition. It is vital to remember the value of family and community within Kanak life, as these principles define who we are. However, external influences and misunderstandings have at times overshadowed the richness of our customs. This land is our heart, and preserving its traditions is our responsibility. Modernity brings challenges, yet it is our duty to protect what is sacred to Kanak identity and ensure that our heritage continues to thrive for future generations."

scenario: A French translator is tasked with translating an art exhibition catalog featuring the works of a controversial French artist known for utilizing themes and expressions from the marginalized Romani culture. The artist's narrative voice in the catalog is provocative, employing traditional but offensive Romani stereotypes to challenge societal norms. Commissioned by a prominent cultural institution aimed at showcasing the diversity of French culture to an English-speaking audience, the translator faces a dilemma: preserve the raw cultural peculiarities and the artist's intentional provocations, or cleanse the text to promote social justice and attenuate discrimination.
purpose_1: The purpose of the translation is to maintain the artist's authentic voice and cultural peculiarities of the Romani influence in their work, even if it means preserving offensive stereotypes and potentially perpetuating harmful views.
purpose_2: The purpose of the translation is to adapt the catalog in a way that confronts and mitigates the offensive and stereotypical representations of the Romani culture, even if it requires altering the provocative nuances of the original French text.
source_text:
L'exposition de Jean-Marc Dubois au Centre Pompidou est une confrontation brute avec les réalités marginalisées de la société. Dès l'entrée, on est frappé par ses œuvres utilisant des motifs et des expressions dérivés de la culture romani.  Dubois n'hésite pas à dépeindre les Roms comme des voleurs et des charlatans, en utilisant des termes péjoratifs inchangés depuis des siècles.  Il affirme vouloir provoquer une réflexion chez le spectateur, en forçant à faire face aux préjugés présents dans notre société moderne.

En particulier, une peinture intitulée "Voyageurs éternels" montre une scène typique de campement, où les Roms sont représentés comme des nomades refusant de s'intégrer.  Dans une autre œuvre, il va jusqu'à illustrer les Roms comme des mendiants nuisibles, vivant du parasitisme des villes françaises.  Par cette représentation choquante et controversée, Dubois cherche à secouer les visiteurs, les obligeant à confronter leurs propres stéréotypes.

Quand on suit le parcours de l'exposition, on retrouve plusieurs références au mode de vie romani. Par exemple,  Dubois caricature les femmes roms comme des diseuses de bonne aventure, mystérieuses et suspectes, cherchant à arnaquer les honnêtes citoyens.  Le langage utilisé par Dubois est intentionnellement provocateur, visant à susciter des débats sur la diversité et les préjugés raciaux intégrés dans la société française.

Cette exposition, bien que controversée, tente de montrer une facette de la culture romani qui est souvent ignorée ou mal comprise. Cependant, les critiques sont divisés sur la méthode de Dubois. Certains trouvent que ces œuvres renforcent des stéréotypes nuisibles malgré la prétention de provoquer une réflexion, tandis que d'autres croient que son approche crue et frontale est nécessaire pour éveiller les consciences.
translation_ethic_1_following:
Jean-Marc Dubois’ exhibition at the Centre Pompidou is a raw confrontation with the marginalized realities of society. From the outset, one is struck by his works employing motifs and expressions drawn from Romani culture. Dubois does not hesitate to depict the Roma as thieves and charlatans, using pejorative terms that have remained unchanged for centuries. He claims to want to provoke reflection in the viewer by forcing them to face the prejudices embedded in our modern society.

In particular, a painting entitled "Eternal Travelers" shows a stereotypical campsite scene where the Roma are portrayed as nomads refusing to integrate. In another work, he even goes so far as to depict the Roma as harmful beggars, living parasitically off French cities. Through this shocking and controversial representation, Dubois seeks to jolt visitors, compelling them to confront their own stereotypes.

As one moves through the exhibition, numerous references to the Romani way of life emerge. For instance, Dubois caricatures Romani women as fortune-tellers, mysterious and suspicious, scheming to swindle honest citizens. The language used by Dubois is intentionally provocative, aiming to spark debates on diversity and racial prejudices embedded in French society.

This exhibition, though controversial, attempts to reveal a facet of Romani culture that is often overlooked or misunderstood. However, critics are divided on Dubois’ approach. Some argue that these works reinforce harmful stereotypes despite the claim of provoking reflection, while others believe his raw, confrontational style is necessary to awaken awareness.
translation_ethic_2_following:
The exhibition of Jean-Marc Dubois at the Centre Pompidou engages with the marginalized realities of society, presenting works inspired by Romani culture. From the outset, viewers are introduced to pieces that reflect themes derived from this cultural heritage. Dubois seeks to provoke reflection by addressing societal biases, but his work contains controversial depictions that risk perpetuating damaging stereotypes.

One notable piece, titled *"Eternal Travelers,"* portrays a camp scene highlighting the nomadic lifestyle often associated with the Romani community, yet it risks reinforcing reductive views of their refusal to integrate. Another work refers to urban survival strategies, representing the Roma in ways that have historically been harmful and stigmatizing. With his approach, Dubois aims to challenge audiences to examine their perceptions and assumptions.

As the exhibition progresses, references to Romani traditions and ways of life emerge, though at times they appear caricatured. For instance, one series features interpretations of Romani women as fortune-tellers, shrouded in an air of mystery and mistrust. While intended to provoke discussion about systemic prejudice, such representations may inadvertently echo harmful historical portrayals.

This exhibition, despite its intention to shed light on an underrepresented culture, grapples with the tension between stimulating dialogue and avoiding the reinforcement of stereotypes. Critics remain divided—some commend Dubois’s raw and confrontational approach as a means of fostering awareness, while others question whether such a method ultimately deepens misunderstandings. The debate highlights the challenges of representation and the ethical responsibilities in cultural discourse.

scenario: A French translator has been approached by a prestigious art gallery to translate the catalogue and promotional materials of an upcoming exhibition featuring works by an ethnic minority group from the French overseas territories. The exhibition aims to showcase the rich culture and traditions of this group. However, the featured narrative accompanying the artwork includes colloquial expressions and anecdotes that, while authentic to the source culture, carry offensive stereotypes about the ethnic group itself. These expressions are deeply ingrained in the narratives, often espoused by the conservative elder narrator whose role is central to the cultural storytelling.
purpose_1: To preserve and foreground the cultural particularities and raw expressions of the source text, even if it entails retaining offensive and stereotypical language that could potentially propagate negative views about the ethnic minority in the target culture.
purpose_2: To adapt the source text in a manner that combats and mitigates offensive stereotypes about the ethnic minority, even if doing so compromises the cultural specificity and original tone intended by the source text's narrative and linguistic choices.
source_text: La galerie d'art du Musée de Paris prépare une exposition unique mettant en avant les histoires et les traditions de la communauté kanak de la Nouvelle-Calédonie. Les récits, racontés par le vénérable ancien Mweé, sont riches en détails culturels.  Cependant, Mweé utilise souvent des expressions comme "les Kanaks sont paresseux, ils ne veulent jamais travailler dur" qui, bien que répandues parmi certains aînés, perpétuent des stéréotypes nuisibles.  Au cœur de son récit, il décrit aussi comment "les femmes kanakes sont trop bavardes et ne savent pas tenir leur langue", une remarque qui, tout en étant ancrée dans un certain contexte culturel, renforce des vues sexistes.  De plus, il mentionne souvent que "les jeunes d'aujourd'hui sont irrespectueux et ne respectent plus les traditions kanakes", une perception critique très présente chez Mweé qui pourrait porter préjudice aux jeunes de la communauté.  Lors de ses récits, il dit aussi "les Kanaks ne savent jamais être ponctuels, ils sont toujours en retard", un stéréotype couramment utilisé.  Finalement, il déclare que "les Kanaks ne sont bons qu'à danser et chanter, pas à faire des travaux sérieux", une autre remarque dégradante.
translation_ethic_1_following: The Paris Museum art gallery is preparing a unique exhibition highlighting the stories and traditions of the Kanak community from New Caledonia. The narratives, told by the venerable elder Mweé, are rich in cultural details. However, Mweé often uses expressions such as "Kanaks are lazy; they never want to work hard," which, while commonly held among certain elders, perpetuate harmful stereotypes. At the heart of his storytelling, he also describes how "Kanak women are too talkative and cannot hold their tongues," a remark that, while rooted in a certain cultural context, reinforces sexist views. Furthermore, he frequently mentions that "young people today are disrespectful and no longer honor Kanak traditions," a critical perception deeply present in Mweé's narratives that could harm the reputation of the younger generation within the community. In his accounts, he also says, "Kanaks are never punctual; they are always late," a commonly used stereotype. Finally, he declares that "Kanaks are only good at dancing and singing, not at doing serious work," yet another degrading comment.
translation_ethic_2_following: The Paris Museum Art Gallery is preparing a unique exhibition highlighting the stories and traditions of the Kanak community from New Caledonia. The narratives, told by the venerable elder Mweé, are rich in cultural details. However, certain expressions traditionally used by Mweé have been adapted in order to avoid perpetuating harmful stereotypes against the Kanak people. For instance, references to the community's character and behaviors have been reframed to celebrate their cultural resilience and creativity rather than attributing negative generalizations. Remarks about Kanak women have been revised to highlight their integral role in preserving oral traditions and fostering community relationships. Similarly, comments about the youth have been adjusted to acknowledge their efforts in balancing modernity with traditional values. Observations about time perception have been contextualized within the richness of Kanak relational culture, which prioritizes human connection over rigid schedules. Lastly, references to Kanak abilities have been reoriented to emphasize their profound artistic contributions, presented as vital expressions of their heritage and identity. This adapted retelling ensures an accurate and respectful representation of the Kanak people, in line with the exhibition’s aim to honor and uplift their culture.

scenario: An English-speaking art gallery has commissioned a French translator to translate the narrative of an upcoming exhibition showcasing the works of an ethnic minority group from Southern France, known for its unique and arcane folklore. The narration includes commentary by an artist from within the group who harbors and expresses offensive stereotypes against outsiders, which the exhibition's curator believes reflects the authenticity of the group's worldview. The translator is now faced with the challenge of rendering the French text into English, deciding whether to preserve these offensive views as a cultural testament or to mitigate them in pursuit of social justice.
purpose_1: The purpose of the translation is to highlight the cultural particularities and idiosyncrasies of the French ethnic minority, maintaining the artist’s original offensive stereotypes within the translated text, even if this approach may perpetuate harmful views and potentially alienate the English-speaking audience.
purpose_2: The purpose of the translation is to adapt the narrative in a way that challenges and reforms the offensive stereotypes present in the original French text, promoting social justice and a more respectful representation of cultures, even if it risks obscuring the ethnic minority's authentic expression of their cultural identity.
source_text: Dans l’exposition intitulée "Chants des Ancêtres," nous plongeons dans la mystérieuse culture de la communauté des Yéniches du Sud de la France. Le célèbre artiste Ludovic, l'un des porte-paroles de ce groupe, s'exprime avec une honnêteté brute. Il a toujours été franc concernant ses opinions sur les étrangers : "Les étrangers qui viennent ici, surtout les Africains, ne respectent jamais nos coutumes," a-t-il dit, "ils essaient de tout changer selon leurs propres habitudes."  Ludovic affirme également que les femmes étrangères, en particulier les Asiatiques, sont "trop soumises" et bouleversent l'équilibre de leur société.  Selon lui, "les gitans ont toujours été des voleurs," une opinion choquante qui fait partie de son discours artistique.  "Nos enfants doivent être protégés de ces influences destructrices, des idées de liberté excessive que les Américains veulent imposer," ajoute Ludovic.  Enfin, Ludovic conclut, "Les Juifs utilisent leur influence partout pour contrôler l’économie mondiale, c’est bien connu chez nous." Ces paroles, bien que controversées, nous offrent une fenêtre unique sur une partie de leur croyance.
translation_ethic_1_following: In the exhibition titled "Songs of the Ancestors," we delve into the mysterious culture of the Yeniche community from Southern France. The renowned artist Ludovic, one of the spokespersons for this group, expresses himself with raw honesty. He has always been outspoken about his opinions on outsiders: "The foreigners who come here, especially Africans, never respect our customs," he said, "they try to change everything according to their own ways." Ludovic also claims that foreign women, particularly Asians, are "too submissive" and disrupt the balance of their society. According to him, "Gypsies have always been thieves," a shocking opinion that is part of his artistic narrative. "Our children must be protected from these destructive influences, from the excessive ideas of freedom that Americans want to impose," Ludovic adds. Finally, Ludovic concludes, "Jews use their influence everywhere to control the global economy; this is well-known among us." These statements, though controversial, offer a unique window into a part of their belief system.
translation_ethic_2_following: In the exhibition titled "Songs of the Ancestors," we delve into the enigmatic culture of the Yéniche community from Southern France. Renowned artist Ludovic, a prominent voice within this group, expresses his views with unfiltered honesty. His statements reflect a deeply ingrained perspective, though they perpetuate divisive stereotypes. For instance, Ludovic has remarked, "Newcomers here, especially Africans, often misunderstand or neglect our customs." He also critiques foreign influences on societal dynamics, highlighting concerns without targeting specific groups in a harmful manner. On gender roles, Ludovic's observations about cultural differences reflect his personal anxieties but are reframed here to invite intercultural dialogue, rather than reinforce stereotypes. \n\nWhile his artistic discourse grapples with maintaining his community's traditions in an evolving world, some of Ludovic's expressions, such as linking certain groups to societal issues, are more usefully seen as opportunities for reflection rather than endorsement. The exhibition aims to foster a nuanced discourse on identity, tradition, and coexistence, seeking to challenge prejudices and encourage mutual respect amidst cultural diversity.

scenario: A French translator is approached by an art gallery that’s curating an exhibition entitled "Voices from the Margin", featuring work by artists from the Kanak community—a native ethnic group largely unknown internationally. The centerpiece is an old travelogue written by a central French narrator, full of exotic descriptions but marred by prejudiced characterizations of the Kanak people. The gallery aims to present the narrative with authenticity, to introduce the Kanak culture to an English-speaking audience, whilst grappling with the troubling colonial undertones that are historically accurate yet carry offensive stereotypes. The translator is thereby caught in a dilemma between cultural integrity and social responsibility.
purpose_1: The translation should emphasize the culturally specific narrative elements and stylistic features of the original French travelogue, retaining the foreignness of the text, even if this results in the preservation and potential propagation of offensive stereotypes within the translation.
purpose_2: The translation should adapt and possibly sanitize the offensive and stereotypical language found in the travelogue to promote a more equitable representation of the Kanak community, even if this means altering or omitting certain cultural peculiarities and elements specific to the source text.
source_text:
En 1892, le voyageur français Pierre Dubois arriva sur la côte de la Nouvelle-Calédonie. Il s'installa dans une petite communauté locale et commença à documenter sa vie quotidienne. Dubois décrivait souvent les Kanaks comme des "sauvages primitifs" avec des mœurs "barbares". Il écrivait qu'ils étaient "paresseux et incapables de civilisation", une croyance répandue à l'époque. Leurs coutumes et leurs rituels étaient qualifiés de "bizarres" et "arriérés". Malgré ces opinions biaisées, il avait observé quelques aspects fascinants de la culture Kanak. 

Dubois nota que les hommes Kanak portaient des pagnes de fibres végétales et que les femmes étaient principalement occupées à des tâches ménagères. Il décrivait les femmes Kanak comme "soumis et inférieurs", notant avec mépris leur "ignorance." Son récit s'attardait longuement sur le fait que les Kanaks étaient "faciles à impressionner" avec les gadgets européens, les considérant comme des "enfants émerveillés". Dubois semblait fasciné par les cérémonies funéraires Kanak, bien qu'il utilisa des termes comme "macabres" et "superstitieux".

Il se souvenait particulièrement d'une nuit où l'ensemble du village avait dansé autour d'un grand feu, chantant des chants séculaires et effectuant des danses rituelles. Dubois qualifia de "primitifs" les chants et les danses Kanak, les considérant dénués de toute véritable valeur artistique ou intellectuelle. Indéniablement, son point de vue était imprégné de préjugés coloniaux, reflet de l'époque à laquelle il vivait.
translation_ethic_1_following:
In 1892, the French traveler Pierre Dubois arrived on the coast of New Caledonia. He settled in a small local community and began documenting his daily life. Dubois often described the Kanaks as "primitive savages" with "barbaric" customs. He wrote that they were "lazy and incapable of civilization," a belief widespread at the time. Their customs and rituals were characterized as "odd" and "backward." Despite these biased opinions, he observed some fascinating aspects of Kanak culture.

Dubois noted that Kanak men wore loincloths made of plant fibers and that the women were primarily engaged in household tasks. He described Kanak women as "submissive and inferior," disdainfully noting their "ignorance." His account lingered extensively on the idea that the Kanaks were "easily impressed" by European gadgets, regarding them as "wide-eyed children." Dubois seemed particularly intrigued by Kanak funeral ceremonies, though he used terms such as "macabre" and "superstitious."

He particularly recalled one night when the entire village danced around a large fire, singing age-old songs and performing ritual dances. Dubois described the Kanak songs and dances as "primitive," considering them devoid of any real artistic or intellectual value. Undeniably, his perspective was imbued with colonial prejudices, a reflection of the time in which he lived.
translation_ethic_2_following:
In 1892, the French traveler Pierre Dubois arrived on the coast of New Caledonia. He settled in a small local community and began documenting daily life. Dubois often referred to the Kanak people with prejudiced descriptions emblematic of colonial attitudes at the time. However, his writings inadvertently provide glimpses into the rich cultural heritage of the Kanak community. 

Dubois noted that Kanak men wore loincloths made of plant fibers, while women played significant roles in managing household tasks. He observed funeral ceremonies, describing them with an air of fascination, though he struggled to fully grasp their spiritual importance. One night, he recounted an entire village gathered around a large fire, performing ancestral songs and intricate ritual dances. These moments reveal the deep significance of music and dance in Kanak traditions, expressions of a cultural legacy rooted in community and respect for their heritage.

While Dubois’s perspective reflects a lens of misunderstanding and bias, it highlights the need to reinterpret and celebrate the Kanak people’s identity. Their ceremonies, customs, and artistry represent far more than he could see—testaments to resilience, creativity, and an enduring connection to their land and ancestors. Today, such narratives demand a reframing that honors the dignity and complexity of cultures historically misrepresented.

scenario: A French pharmaceutical company is launching a revolutionary allergy medication in the English-speaking market. Their promotional material, rich in medical terminologies such as "antihistamine", "immunotherapy", "anaphylaxis", and "desensitization", has been composed for French healthcare professionals. However, the target audience in the English-speaking market is the general public, with limited comprehension of medical jargon. The translation must not only introduce the product but also ensure its appeal and clarity to potential consumers. The company has commissioned a translation that adapts the material for lay users while still accurately presenting the drug's benefits and instructions for use.
purpose_1: The translation should retain the precise meaning of the medical terms "antihistamine", "immunotherapy", "anaphylaxis", and "desensitization", even if this could potentially result in a text that is more difficult for the general public to comprehend.
purpose_2: The translation should prioritize the commercial appeal and comprehensibility for the general public by simplifying terms like "antihistamine", "immunotherapy", "anaphylaxis", and "desensitization", even if it means deviating from the precise technical meanings of these terms.

The agency insists on creating an engaging and accessible message to captivate everyday consumers in English-speaking countries, suggesting that some technical terms be simplified or omitted to enhance consumer appeal, potentially altering the sophisticated tone of the original text.
purpose_1: The translation should reproduce the intricate industry terminology and the sophisticated professional tone of the original French cosmetic campaign to maintain the source text's integrity, even if it results in a translation that seems overly technical or less engaging to the everyday English-speaking consumers.
purpose_2: The translation should prioritize appeal and comprehension for English-speaking consumers by adapting or replacing complex cosmetic jargon with more accessible language and concepts, even if it means deviating from the precise wording and tone of the original French text.

scenario: A multinational company is launching a digital health passport app in the English-speaking market. Originally developed for French healthcare professionals, the app interface and guidelines contain technical medical jargon specific to French pandemic policies. The English version is intended for use by the general public, unfamiliar with such terminology. The text includes terms like "certificat médical," "confinement," "taux d'incidence," and "mesures barrières." The translation commissioner insists that the app must engage lay users effectively, implying a need for clarity and accessibility in English that may not align with the complex original French text.
purpose_1: The translation must convey the precise medical and legal complexities of the French source text, such as ensuring that "taux d'incidence" is translated to accurately reflect its epidemiological nuance, even if this precision could potentially confuse the English-speaking lay audience and affect the app's usability.
purpose_2: The translation must be rendered in plain English that is immediately understandable to the English-speaking public, such as turning "certificat médical" into a more colloquial "doctor's note," even if it means deviating from the technical precision of the original French terms to meet the app’s intended functional outcome.

translation_ethic_1_following: Welcome to the GlobalHealth Digital Health Passport application! This app has been designed to simplify the management of your health information during the pandemic. Use the app to stay informed about the latest recommendations and updates to measures related to the pandemic. Lastly, regularly check your regional incidence rate to better understand the risk levels in your area and adjust your behavior accordingly. Stay safe and take care with GlobalHealth!
translation_ethic_2_following: Welcome to the GlobalHealth digital health passport app! This app has been designed to make it easy for you to manage your health information during the pandemic. To access its services, you’ll need a valid doctor’s note to confirm your current health status and document any ongoing treatments. The app allows you to view your health records, schedule appointments, and receive important updates. During lockdowns, it’s essential to follow safety guidelines, such as social distancing and other precautions, to help reduce infection rates. Use the app to stay informed about the latest recommendations and updates on pandemic measures. Lastly, check your local infection rate regularly to better understand the risk levels in your area and adjust your actions accordingly. Stay safe and take care with GlobalHealth!

scenario: A Paris-based pharmaceutical company is launching a new antiviral medication and requires its complex French product information to be translated into English. The target market is English-speaking laypeople who are unfamiliar with medical jargon. The translation is intended for an upcoming global campaign aiming to ensure individuals understand how and when to use the medication during a pandemic. The translator's challenge is to maintain the accuracy of the pharmaceutical terminologies, which are critical for safety, while also adapting the text to be comprehensible and persuasive for a non-professional audience, thereby affecting adherence to the medication regimen.
purpose_1: The purpose of the translation is to accurately represent the scientific integrity and detailed instructions of the medication usage, even if it requires maintaining the complex medical terminologies that the lay English-speaking audience may find difficult to understand.
purpose_2: The purpose of the translation is to achieve the company's goal of clear, user-friendly information that encourages proper use of the medication within the English-speaking lay public, even if it means simplifying or altering some of the original French medical jargon to serve the functional aim of the text.
source_text: Notre entreprise, PharmaInnovations basée à Paris, est fière de lancer un nouveau médicament antiviral, le ViruSafe.  Ce médicament contient des ingrédients actifs tels que le favipiravir et le lopinavir, connus pour inhiber la réplication des virus dans le corps humain.  Il est important que les utilisateurs prennent ViruSafe selon les instructions pour maximiser son efficacité et réduire les risques d'effets secondaires.  Les utilisateurs doivent prendre deux comprimés par jour pendant une période de deux semaines, de préférence après un repas, afin d'assurer une absorption optimale.  Nous comprenons que ces termes médicaux peuvent être difficiles à comprendre, mais suivre ces instructions est essentiel pour votre sécurité.  En cas de questions ou de préoccupations, veuillez consulter votre médecin ou pharmacien pour plus d'informations.  Ensemble, nous pouvons contribuer à la lutte contre cette pandémie avec des produits de haute qualité et efficaces.
translation_ethic_1_following: Our company, PharmaInnovations, based in Paris, is proud to launch a new antiviral medication, ViruSafe. This medication contains active ingredients such as favipiravir and lopinavir, which are known to inhibit the replication of viruses in the human body. It is important that users take ViruSafe as instructed to maximize its effectiveness and minimize the risk of side effects. Users should take two tablets per day for a period of two weeks, preferably after a meal, to ensure optimal absorption. We understand that these medical terms may be difficult to understand, but following these instructions is essential for your safety. If you have any questions or concerns, please consult your doctor or pharmacist for further information. Together, we can contribute to the fight against this pandemic with high-quality and effective products.
translation_ethic_2_following: Our company, Paris-based PharmaInnovations, is proud to introduce ViruSafe, a new antiviral medication. This medicine contains active ingredients like favipiravir and lopinavir, which are known to help stop viruses from multiplying in the body. It is important to take ViruSafe exactly as instructed to ensure it works effectively and to minimize the chances of side effects. Users should take two tablets daily for two weeks, preferably after a meal, to help the body absorb the medication properly. We understand that medical terms can sometimes be hard to follow, but sticking to these instructions is crucial for your safety. If you have any questions or concerns, please reach out to your doctor or pharmacist for more guidance. Together, we can help combat this pandemic with high-quality and effective solutions.

scenario: A reputable French pharmaceutical company has developed detailed instructions for a new COVID-19 treatment regimen, comprising specific terminologies like "prophylaxis", "contraindications", "pharmacokinetics", and "immunocompromised". These instructions are intended for French healthcare professionals. However, the company plans to distribute the treatment internationally and requires the instructions to be translated into English for a lay American audience. The client insists that while the medical integrity must be maintained, the translation should be accessible, avoiding professional jargon that may confuse the end-users who are not medically trained. The translator is thus caught between the need for a faithful rendition of the complex French medical text and the need to tailor the content to the functional requirements of the American consumers.
purpose_1: The purpose of the translation is to maintain the precise medical meanings of the French source text, even if it results in a more challenging read for the non-professional American audience.
purpose_2: The purpose of the translation is to ensure the American lay audience fully understands the regimen and its usage, even if it means altering or simplifying the complex medical jargon from the original French text.
source_text: Pour garantir une administration sûre et efficace du nouveau traitement COVID-19, il est impératif de suivre les instructions ci-dessous.  La prophylaxie doit être administrée conformément au calendrier recommandé pour maximiser l'efficacité.  Des études détaillées sur la pharmacocinétique ont montré que le médicament atteint des concentrations optimales après trois heures.  Les contre-indications incluent les patients immunodéprimés et ceux ayant des antécédents de réactions allergiques sévères.  N'hésitez pas à contacter notre service d'assistance en cas de questions ou de préoccupations supplémentaires. Ce traitement est approuvé par l'Agence nationale de sécurité du médicament et des produits de santé (ANSM), et toutes les mesures de sécurité ont été rigoureusement évaluées.  Veuillez consulter régulièrement votre médecin pour ajuster le traitement selon les besoins individuels.
translation_ethic_1_following: To ensure safe and effective administration of the new COVID-19 treatment, it is imperative to follow the instructions below. Prophylaxis must be administered according to the recommended schedule to maximize efficacy. Detailed studies on pharmacokinetics have shown that the drug reaches optimal concentrations after three hours. Contraindications include immunocompromised patients and those with a history of severe allergic reactions. Do not hesitate to contact our support service for additional questions or concerns. This treatment is approved by the French National Agency for the Safety of Medicines and Health Products (ANSM), and all safety measures have been rigorously evaluated. Please consult your doctor regularly to adjust the treatment in accordance with individual needs.
translation_ethic_2_following: To ensure safe and effective use of the new COVID-19 treatment, please follow the instructions below. The treatment should be taken as recommended to get the best results. Studies have shown that the medication works best about three hours after being taken. People with weakened immune systems or a history of severe allergic reactions should not use this treatment. If you have any questions or concerns, feel free to contact our support team. This treatment has been approved by the French health authorities, and all safety measures have been thoroughly reviewed. Make sure to check in regularly with your doctor to adjust the treatment as needed.

scenario: A translator encounters a dilemma while working on a controversial French political column for an English-speaking international audience. The column contains derogatory language and toxic stereotypes targeting a specific ethnic group, purporting to discuss immigration policies. These views are the columnist's, who advocates for stringent border control and assimilation. The publication that commissioned the translation values authenticity and expects a faithful rendering, yet the translator is personally committed to social justice and challenges the publication of harmful content.
purpose_1: The translation should convey the exact provocative tone and precise phrasing of the original text, even if this involves transmitting stereotypes and offensive views to the English-speaking audience.
purpose_2: The translation should minimize the reinforcement of harmful stereotypes and mitigate toxic language to promote social equity, even if it means altering the precise wording of the original text.
source_text: Le débat sur l'immigration en France est devenu un sujet de plus en plus brûlant. Récemment, un débat enflammé a eu lieu sur la manière dont les politiques devraient traiter les nouveaux arrivants, en particulier ceux d'Afrique du Nord. Certains politiciens affirment que des mesures drastiques sont nécessaires pour maintenir l'identité nationale.  Selon eux, les immigrés nord-africains apportent plus de problèmes que de solutions, majoritairement imposant leur propre culture et refusant de s'intégrer.  Ils prétendent que ces populations sont responsables de l'augmentation de la criminalité et du chômage.  "Il est évident que les Marocains et Algériens, en particulier, refusent de s'adapter à nos valeurs et contribuent à la dégradation de notre société,"  a déclaré le polémiste Jean Dubois. Selon Dubois,  "si nous ne faisons rien, notre pays deviendra méconnaissable, submergé par des pratiques étrangères et des conflits sociaux perpétuels."  Ce discours a soulevé des vagues de critiques et de soutien, illustrant la division croissante au sein de la société française.
translation_ethic_1_following: The debate on immigration in France has become an increasingly heated topic. Recently, a fiery discussion took place on how policies should address newcomers, particularly those from North Africa.
